title: phaseolus maculatus subsp. ritensis
intro: Phaseolus maculatus subsp. ritensis, also known as the wild mottled bean or variegated bean, is a subspecies of the Phaseolus maculatus plant, which is a type of wild bean native to parts of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Phaseolus maculatus subsp. ritensis include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used as a natural remedy for various ailments.
